interpretation: Dravidian z=17.35 vs Sanskrit z=52.72 → 0.33× preference. This is the key quantitative falsification of the competing Sanskrit hypothesis.
methodological_note: z-score comparison is INVALID when LMs have different vocabulary sizes (Dravidian 15,426 bigrams vs Sanskrit 651 bigrams). Sparse LM produces lower null variance → inflated z. Use lift ratio instead.
verdict_corrected: DRAVIDIAN_PREFERRED (lift basis): Dravidian 22.4% lift vs Sanskrit 12.6% lift = 1.78x. z-score comparison invalid (LM size mismatch). Phase-44 3.13x (same-baseline) remains definitive.
